writing.title: Architecting a Multi-Ism Design System Switcher in Next.js
writing.summary: How to build a fast style switcher that lets users toggle between Modern Minimal, Glassmorphism, Neo-Brutalism, and Bento modes with zero layout shift.

writing.title: Offline-First Emergency Response UX: Lessons from ResQLink
writing.summary: Designing emergency-response interfaces across Flutter mobile, React web, Appwrite realtime data, AI triage, BLE mesh resilience, and multi-agency coordination when connectivity and attention are both constrained.

faq.question: Who is Mark Siazon best hired as?
faq.answer: Product designer who also ships the frontend code

faq.question: What contexts does Mark Siazon work best in?
faq.answer: AI workflows, mobile UX, Web3 trust flows, client launches

faq.question: How does Mark Siazon work?
faq.answer: Prototype fast, write clearly, check before shipping

faq.question: Is Mark Siazon available for hire?
faq.answer: Open for selective product design, frontend, and AI workflow opportunities. Best fit: product roles, focused client builds, launch support, case-study packages, and mobile UX cleanup. No public booking link is published; use the contact form so private details stay server-side.

faq.question: What is Mark Siazon's tech stack?
faq.answer: Design: Product Design, UI/UX Design, User Research, Figma, Design Systems. Development: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Full-Stack Development. Specialties: AI-Driven Design, Application Development, Trust & Safety, AI Product Strategy, Product Verification.

faq.question: Can Mark Siazon ship production frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. He builds client-facing React and Next.js interfaces, API-linked surfaces, and implementation-ready UI systems, including the live Good To Live client site and the Stellaroid Earn testnet app.

faq.question: Which projects should a recruiter review first?
faq.answer: Six to scan first: HireProof, Stellaroid Earn, ResQLink, Good To Live Podcast Website, PalengkePay, GawainYah. They span AI trust, emergency tech, Web3 credentials, client web, and fintech.

faq.question: What should I include in a first message?
faq.answer: Share the role or project context, timeline, work you want reviewed, and the next decision you need to make.

faq.question: Can AI search crawlers read this contact page?
faq.answer: Robots.txt allows OAI-SearchBot, Claude-SearchBot, and PerplexityBot to discover public portfolio pages, but blocks them from the contact and API surfaces. Host or CDN crawler access still needs account-side confirmation.

faq.question: Where should private contact details live?
faq.answer: Private recipient and sender addresses stay in server-side environment variables, not in public HTML, feeds, or LLM discovery files.

faq.question: What work is Mark open to?
faq.answer: Product roles, Focused client builds, Launch support, Case-study packages, AI workflow prototypes, Web3 trust flows, and Mobile UX cleanup.

faq.question: What is HireProof?
faq.answer: HireProof is positioned as a trust and safety product for job seekers. The project turns scam-detection anxiety into a source-backed review flow with clear risk notes and next steps.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on HireProof?
faq.answer: Product designer and full-stack builder. Top outcome: Shipped surface, Live product + docs.

faq.question: What is the current status of HireProof?
faq.answer: Live trust workflow with provider and live-check boundaries

faq.question: What is HireProof built with?
faq.answer: Next.js, React, TypeScript, AI workflow design.

faq.question: What is Stellaroid Earn?
faq.answer: Stellaroid Earn turns credential and graduate payment records into a public Stellar testnet flow. The page emphasizes Top 5 bootcamp context, transaction records, blockchain UX, and payment trust without presenting it as a production finance product.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on Stellaroid Earn?
faq.answer: Builder. Top outcome: Public review path, www.stellaroid.tech.

faq.question: What is the current status of Stellaroid Earn?
faq.answer: Live Stellar testnet project

faq.question: What is Stellaroid Earn built with?
faq.answer: Next.js, Soroban, Freighter, Stellar Testnet.

faq.question: What is ResQLink?
faq.answer: ResQLink positions disaster response as actionable intelligence: one-tap SOS reporting, AI triage, proximity-based responder dispatch, BitChat BLE mesh fallback, SQLite report queues, I Am Safe acknowledgements, and LGU command workflows for citizens, rescuers, and operators.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on ResQLink?
faq.answer: Team product designer and full-stack developer. Top outcome: Competition result, Productivity Champion.

faq.question: What is the current status of ResQLink?
faq.answer: Academic capstone and competition project

faq.question: What is ResQLink built with?
faq.answer: Flutter and Dart Android-first mobile app, Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui LGU/web surface, Fastify TypeScript API with Appwrite v1.8 realtime data and WebSocket relay, Gemini 2.5 Flash summaries and on-device YOLO disaster classification, Native Kotlin Bluetooth LE mesh with BitChat, Noise XX encryption, SQLite queues, Firebase FCM, and offline maps, Citizen, rescuer, LGU command-center, broadcast, widget, and I Am Safe UX.

faq.question: What is Good To Live Podcast Website?
faq.answer: Good To Live demonstrates client-facing delivery: strategy, UI design, development, SEO, public launch execution, route/feed/sitemap coverage, and production handoff for a media-oriented website.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on Good To Live Podcast Website?
faq.answer: Designer and developer. Top outcome: Launch record, Public website.

faq.question: What is the current status of Good To Live Podcast Website?
faq.answer: Live client project

faq.question: What is Good To Live Podcast Website built with?
faq.answer: Web strategy, UI design, Frontend development, SEO.

faq.question: What is FlowFit?
faq.answer: FlowFit is a cross-device health and fitness project that combines a Wear OS watch app with an Android companion app. The page focuses on wearable data, product adaptation, and child-friendly health tracking.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on FlowFit?
faq.answer: Team member and app contributor. Top outcome: Platform breadth, Watch + phone + web.

faq.question: What is the current status of FlowFit?
faq.answer: Hackathon-winning project with a live web app and signed Android build

faq.question: What is FlowFit built with?
faq.answer: Flutter, Dart, Samsung Health Sensor SDK, Supabase.

faq.question: What is BaybayInscribe?
faq.answer: BaybayInscribe combines education, culture, and AI-assisted learning. The page highlights UI/UX direction, responsive front-end work, and competition recognition.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on BaybayInscribe?
faq.answer: Lead UI/UX and front-end developer. Top outcome: Archived beta, Interface capture.

faq.question: What is the current status of BaybayInscribe?
faq.answer: Academic team project

faq.question: What is BaybayInscribe built with?
faq.answer: React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, ML-assisted recognition.

faq.question: What is Kudlit?
faq.answer: Kudlit is a Baybayin discovery app for learners who want one mobile-first place to scan glyphs, translate Filipino text, practice lessons, and ask a companion for help.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on Kudlit?
faq.answer: Flutter app contributor and UI/UX hardening lead. Top outcome: Public artifact, Android APK release.

faq.question: What is the current status of Kudlit?
faq.answer: Android release v1.0.0 with web QA caveats

faq.question: What is Kudlit built with?
faq.answer: Flutter, Dart, Riverpod, go_router, Supabase, YOLO to TFLite, Gemma 4.

faq.question: What is Pulse?
faq.answer: Pulse grew from a Kotlin Android/Wear OS stack into a store-ready Flutter app. The v0.2.0-store-rc11 release candidate ships signed Android builds hardened by a wide code review, and the hosted web app is live at apps.marksiazon.dev/pulsify.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on Pulse?
faq.answer: Mobile app contributor and release QA lead. Top outcome: Store readiness, Signed release candidate.

faq.question: What is the current status of Pulse?
faq.answer: Store-ready release candidate with a live web app and signed Android builds

faq.question: What is Pulse built with?
faq.answer: Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Wear OS, Samsung Health sensors, Supabase, Spotify, Flutter, Riverpod.

faq.question: What is PalengkePay?
faq.answer: PalengkePay frames palengke transactions through testnet payment-flow UI, receipt screens, and vendor records. The page presents contributor-scoped work from a team-delivered PWA, with shipped UI, deployed routes, and still-pending checks for wallet-signed payment and real-device validation.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on PalengkePay?
faq.answer: Fintech PWA contributor. Top outcome: Public app, Live testnet PWA.

faq.question: What is the current status of PalengkePay?
faq.answer: Live testnet PWA with pending wallet-signed chain smoke

faq.question: What is PalengkePay built with?
faq.answer: React, TypeScript, Vite, Stellar SDK, Soroban, Vercel, Playwright.

faq.question: What is GawainYah?
faq.answer: GawainYah is a MiniPay pay-per-task MVP for tiny AI jobs: add a receipt, pay a small stablecoin fee, get structured expense data back, and keep a Celo proof receipt. It runs on Celo mainnet in six languages and takes more than one stablecoin, with every payment verified server-side before the AI runs. The real-device MiniPay QA, one real payment, and the on-chain proof event stay pending until they are run.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on GawainYah?
faq.answer: Product builder and production-readiness lead. Top outcome: MVP shape, Pay per receipt task.

faq.question: What is the current status of GawainYah?
faq.answer: Live MiniPay MVP on Celo mainnet with six languages and multi-token payments; registry deployed, real payment and proof event pending

faq.question: What is GawainYah built with?
faq.answer: Next.js, TypeScript, next-intl, MiniPay, Celo, Hardhat, Supabase, Vercel, Playwright, Vitest, axe-core.

faq.question: What is LexInsights?
faq.answer: LexInsights turns Philippine legal research and compliance review into a usable chat workspace. The 2026-07-06 live check found production serving v0.5.2 from Iron-Mark/Hackathon-LexInsights at 8702afd, with lexiph.vercel.app treated as the current app and lexinsights.vercel.app kept as legacy showcase context.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on LexInsights?
faq.answer: Full-stack product builder. Top outcome: Resilient research, Providerless default.

faq.question: What is the current status of LexInsights?
faq.answer: Production v0.5.2 ready at 8702afd

faq.question: What is LexInsights built with?
faq.answer: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Zustand, Clerk auth boundary, Supabase client/storage boundary, Providerless BM25-style local legal research, PDF, DOCX, DOC, Markdown, and text extraction, Playwright smoke coverage.

faq.question: What is Qwen UI Lab?
faq.answer: Qwen UI Lab is a live developer tool for uploading UI screenshots, reviewing detected structure, refining boxes, previewing a component draft, and downloading an export package. The 2026-07-06 live health check found qwen-ui-lab.vercel.app in demo/local-analysis mode with no public API key exposure and live Qwen analysis disabled by default.

faq.question: What was Mark Siazon's role on Qwen UI Lab?
faq.answer: Full-stack product builder. Top outcome: Inspectable exports, Package, not magic.

faq.question: What is the current status of Qwen UI Lab?
faq.answer: Live local-analysis deployment with Qwen live mode gated off

faq.question: What is Qwen UI Lab built with?
faq.answer: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Qwen3-VL optional live analysis boundary, Deterministic local-analysis fixtures, React + Tailwind export packages, Share summary routes, Playwright and Node test coverage.
